author: y4rr
content: Context: filed while closing #484. The einops rearrange/repeat/reduce refactor in ferrotorch-core/src/einops.rs added GPU-aware fast paths for the common cases (identity-permutation rearrange via view_reshape; axis-aligned reduce via sum_dim/cummax/cummin), but the general case still falls back to a CPU round-trip because Tensor::contiguous() materializes non-contiguous CUDA tensors via data_vec(). The missing primitive is a GPU strided→contiguous gather kernel that takes (input_buf, src_strides, src_offset, output_shape) and writes a contiguous output buffer entirely on-device. Once this exists: (1) wire it into methods::contiguous_t for the CUDA path, (2) the einops fallback in rearrange_with and the legacy reduce loop will both become GPU-native automatically, (3) repeat() can use unsqueeze+expand+contiguous instead of its current CPU index-walk. PTX kernel sketch: each thread handles one output element, decomposes the flat output index into per-axis coordinates via the contiguous output strides, then computes src_offset = sum_d coords[d] * src_strides[d]. Pass shape and strides as small device buffers, support up to 8 dims to keep the kernel general.
